title: Fix wrong Containerfile location on build
body:
Podman does select the wrong Containerfile if the current working
directory contains a Containerfile but we specify one from a different
location.

Reproducer:
```
> mkdir 1
> echo FROM scratch > Containerfile
> echo FROM golang > 1/Containerfile
> podman build -f 1/Containerfile -t test
STEP 1: FROM scratch
```

comments.body:
@TomSweeneyRedHat @QiWang19 PTAL
@saschagrunert need to remove the err check.  I am a little worried this code was added for a reason.

> @TomSweeneyRedHat @QiWang19 PTAL
> @saschagrunert need to remove the err check. I am a little worried this code was added for a reason.

Looks like the code has been added with this commit: https://github.com/containers/libpod/commit/a824186ac9803ef5f7548df790988a4ebd2d9c07

It might be that the buildah implementation has changed which broke the behavior for podman :thinking:
Buildah grew support for Containerfile recently.
Changes LGTM. The `containerfiles[i], err = filepath.Rel(contextDir, absFile)` in the original code seems unnecessay.
